pattern: A merge sort that counts how many right-half elements jump ahead of each left-half element accumulates, across all merges, exactly the number of smaller elements to each element's right.
hints: A naive count compares every element with all those after it; the work is in doing those comparisons faster.
When you merge two sorted halves, every time you take an element from the right half before a given left element, that right element is smaller and was originally to its right.
Sort indices rather than values so you can attribute the counts back to original positions, and add the number of right-half elements already emitted whenever you place a left-half element.

edge_inputs.description: single element has nothing to its right
equal values do not count each other as smaller
mixed array with a clear descending influence
strictly decreasing yields a descending count

stress.description: large random array exercising the merge-count across many inversions
stress.note: wide value range with repeats stresses the equal-value tie handling

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def countSmaller(self, nums):
        n = len(nums)
        res = [0] * n
        idx = list(range(n))
        def sort(lo, hi):
            if hi - lo <= 1:
                return
            mid = (lo + hi) // 2
            sort(lo, mid)
            sort(mid, hi)
            merged = []
            i, j = lo, mid
            right_done = 0
            while i < mid and j < hi:
                if nums[idx[j]] < nums[idx[i]]:
                    right_done += 1
                    merged.append(idx[j])
                    j += 1
                else:
                    res[idx[i]] += right_done
                    merged.append(idx[i])
                    i += 1
            while i < mid:
                res[idx[i]] += right_done
                merged.append(idx[i])
                i += 1
            while j < hi:
                merged.append(idx[j])
                j += 1
            idx[lo:hi] = merged
        sort(0, n)
        return res
